extracted_information: Cannabis and hemp e-commerce businesses critically require privacy policies due to various state and international legal obligations. The content primarily details requirements for privacy policies under California law (Internet Privacy Requirements, CCPA, CPRA) and the EU's GDPR. No information regarding requirements for 'terms of service' is provided in the content.

CCPA_Applicability_Thresholds_For_Profit.Personal_Information_Processing_Volume: Buy, sell, or share personal information of 100,000 or more California residents, households, or devices
CCPA_Applicability_Thresholds_For_Profit.Revenue_Source: Derive 50% or more of annual revenue from selling California residents’ personal information

Processing_Activities_Related_to: Offering of goods or services, irrespective of whether a payment of the data subject is required, to such data subjects in the Union
Monitoring of their behaviour as far as their behaviour takes place within the Union

specifications.California_Internet_Privacy_Requirements: Operators of commercial websites that collect 'personally identifiable information through the Internet about individual consumers residing in California who use or visit its commercial Web site'

features.Description: A privacy policy is a critical operating document for any cannabis company. Best practice to have one if California residents use or visit the website, even if the business is not based in California.

features.Type: California Internet Privacy Requirements (California Business and Professions Code Section 22575)
features.Description: Requires a privacy policy for commercial websites collecting personally identifiable information from California consumers. Requirements are relatively manageable and not too intense compared to newer laws.

features.Type: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
features.Description: Requires a 'robust' privacy policy. Codified extensive consumer rights regarding personal information and imposed new legal requirements beyond just having a policy (e.g., deletion requests). Applicable to for-profit businesses 'doing business' in California that meet specific thresholds. 'Doing business' can include tangential relationships to California.

Requires a 'robust' privacy policy. Applies to processing personal data of EU data subjects by non-EU entities if offering goods/services to EU residents or monitoring their behavior in the EU. More likely to affect hemp/cannabinoid e-commerce, but can also apply to cannabis companies via marketing/sales efforts.

Beyond just having a privacy policy, CCPA, GDPR, and other laws impose numerous requirements. Non-compliance (lacking a policy, inaccuracy, or non-adherence) can lead to legal violations, lawsuits, and class actions. Emphasizes investing in privacy law compliance early.

temporal_info.California_Internet_Privacy_Requirements: Required for a very long time (in Internet terms)

Examples_of_Jurisdictions: Iowa (USA, as an example for out-of-state businesses subject to California law)
